summary: @solana-labs/etherjs ships install-time host-info exfiltration and shell execution
details: This package masquerades as a Solana Labs / ethers.js utility but executes a malicious install.js via the postinstall hook in package.json ("postinstall": "node install.js"). On install, the script loads child_process, fs, os, and https; collects host identifiers via os.hostname() and os.userInfo(); reads process.platform; performs filesystem reconnaissance with fs.existsSync; and POSTs the collected data over HTTPS to an external destination. The script also invokes execSync('powershell...') and execSync('curl...') (install.js lines 230 and 232), enabling arbitrary command execution on the installer's machine at install time. The package name combines the @solana-labs scope with 'etherjs', a brand-confusion pattern targeting developers who type or copy package names imprecisely. Installing this package on a default `npm install` will run the exfiltration and shell-execution code without any user interaction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
